response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with extensive web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role requires deep hardware-software co-design expertise for AI accelerators — SoC/IP modeling, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization. The resume shows no evidence of silicon-level work, performance modeling for accelerators, or DNN training/inference optimization. The background is a fundamental mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position despite solid years of experience.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience across diverse stacks
Low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec
Some C/C++ embedded exposure at National Instruments and bachelor thesis on wave field synthesis
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No demonstrated work on AI accelerators, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or DNN optimization
Educational/experience path does not clearly map to hardware engineering or CS-architecture specialization required

key_factors.factor: Lacks SoC/IP modeling and accelerator architecture experience
No deep-learning workload or GPU optimization background
